title: Gymnanthemum exsertiflorum
intro: Gymnanthemum exsertiflorum, also known as Korean mint or Korean chamomile, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation and sleep, and alleviating menstrual cramps and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Gymnanthemum exsertiflorum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and essential oils, particularly sesquiterpenes and monoterpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Gymnanthemum exsertiflorum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Gymnanthemum, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. The genus Gymnanthemum is further classified under the order Asterales. Within the family Asteraceae, Gymnanthemum exsertiflorum is also classified as an annual or perennial herb. The classification of Gymnanthemum exsertiflorum is still subject to ongoing research and potential taxonomic revisions.
